title: The One with Ross's Library Book
weakest_section: 21:30-22:50 range with resolution but fewer laughs

jokes.text: My God. Sorry about that. Okay, really good.
jokes.setup: Friend asks how Joey's date went, he says 'pretty good'
jokes.punchline: He clearly had loud sex and is awkwardly apologizing to his roommate
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey sexual obliviousness played for cringe. Well-executed physical implication through dialogue.

jokes.text: Just casually slip it in, you know. Lay the groundwork. Tell her I'm a loner. No, an outlaw.
jokes.setup: Joey wants his roommate to tell his date he's not looking for a relationship
jokes.punchline: He escalates from 'loner' to 'outlaw' as if he's a Western movie character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his romantic self-delusion escalating to absurd Western fantasy.

jokes.text: She doesn't want to get mixed up with the likes of me.
jokes.setup: Joey continuing his outlaw fantasy
jokes.punchline: Using old-timey Western dialogue about himself unironically
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the Western outlaw bit. Good character consistency but less impact than the initial escalation.

jokes.text: Can't I tell her that you're a pig?
jokes.setup: Joey asking Chandler to memorize a complex romantic backstory
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests the simpler, more honest approach
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical comeback. Classic Chandler sarcasm cutting through Joey's nonsense.

jokes.text: Chandler used to do it. He'd make her pancakes. He'd make extras and leave them for me.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why Chandler should help with his girl problems
jokes.punchline: Reveals his real motivation was getting free pancakes from Chandler's dates
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey selfishness reveal. The misdirection from relationship help to food motivation is very on-brand.

jokes.text: Now, where did we land on those pancakes?
jokes.setup: After refusing to help Joey with his relationship problem
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately pivots back to the pancake issue
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that reinforces Joey's food obsession. Shows his single-minded focus.

jokes.text: Joey kind of disabled it when I moved in.
jokes.setup: Erin apologizing for not being able to work the door lock
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Joey broke the lock, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Subtle character detail delivered deadpan. Efficient world-building humor about Joey's incompetence.

jokes.text: Would you like some pancakes?
jokes.setup: Erin asking if Joey talked about her
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately offers pancakes instead of answering, referencing Joey's earlier obsession
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that shows Rachel trying to deflect the awkward question while satisfying Joey's pancake request.

jokes.text: ...you know, for fun. And I typed mine in, and guess what came up. My doctoral dissertation.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why they're at the library
jokes.punchline: His idea of 'fun' is typing names into library computers, and he's excited about finding his own academic work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross nerdiness - finding his dissertation is genuinely exciting to him. Great character-specific humor.

jokes.text: There's also a book here by a woman named Wendy Bagina.
jokes.setup: Ross continuing to share his library computer discoveries
jokes.punchline: Name that sounds like 'when the vagina' - juvenile humor delivered earnestly by Ross
jokes.explanation: Cheap juvenile humor, but works because Ross delivers it so innocently. Low craft score for the lazy wordplay.

jokes.text: Sounds like two people are really enjoying the Dewey Decimal System.
jokes.setup: Sexual sounds coming from the library stacks
jokes.punchline: Joey's euphemistic reference to sex using library terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism that combines the library setting with sexual innuendo. More sophisticated than expected from Joey.

jokes.text: You didn't bring me here to do that, did you?
jokes.setup: Hearing people having sex in the library
jokes.punchline: Joey realizes Ross might have brought him here for sexual purposes
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment but fairly predictable. Joey's sexual confusion is in character but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Send the colonel in.
jokes.setup: Waitress says difficult customer wants to explain how she wants her chicken cooked
jokes.punchline: Monica refers to the customer as 'the colonel' (like Colonel Sanders)
jokes.explanation: Cute chicken reference that shows Monica's chef competitiveness. Solid but not exceptional wordplay.

jokes.text: Lucky bastard.
jokes.setup: Monica screams 'Oh my God' when she sees the difficult customer
jokes.punchline: Chandler assumes Monica's excited reaction is positive when it's actually horror
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience and Chandler both misread Monica's reaction. Economical and well-timed.

jokes.text: How are you... Ms. Hotshot-Chef-at-the-Big-Fancy-Restaurant-With-the-Best-Chicken-Ever?
jokes.setup: Janice greeting Monica at her restaurant
jokes.punchline: Janice's trademark over-the-top, run-on greeting with passive-aggressive undertones
jokes.explanation: Pure Janice - the over-dramatic, faux-sweet delivery is perfectly in character. High impact due to character recognition.

jokes.text: Oh, what is that on your finger? I'm blind.
jokes.setup: Janice noticing Monica's engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Claims the ring is so sparkly it's blinding her
jokes.explanation: Classic Janice hyperbole. The dramatic blindness claim is exactly her style of over-reaction.

jokes.text: Somebody's been doing a little cooking outside the restaurant.
jokes.setup: Janice commenting on Monica's engagement
jokes.punchline: Using cooking metaphor for Monica's romantic life
jokes.explanation: Decent cooking pun that fits both Janice's personality and Monica's profession. Serviceable humor.

jokes.text: Funny story.
jokes.setup: Janice asking who Monica's lucky guy is
jokes.punchline: Monica's flat delivery suggests the story is anything but funny
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment that sets up the awkwardness of having to tell Janice about Chandler. Economy of language works well.

jokes.text: Look at that, we're all gonna call each other.
jokes.setup: Joey and Erin having an awkward goodbye with multiple promises to call
jokes.punchline: Rachel pointing out the absurdity of their over-eager phone call promises
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about awkward dating rituals. Rachel cutting through the BS with dry commentary.

jokes.text: The system kind of broke down, huh?
jokes.setup: The plan was for Rachel to tell Erin that Joey wasn't looking for a relationship
jokes.punchline: Joey understating the obvious failure of their plan
jokes.explanation: Nice understated observation from Joey. Shows he's not completely oblivious to social dynamics.

jokes.text: Who do you think brought her here? Cupid.
jokes.setup: Joey questioning why Rachel wants him to marry Erin
jokes.punchline: Rachel earnestly crediting Cupid for their meeting
jokes.explanation: Rachel's romantic delusion is cute but not particularly clever. The Cupid reference feels a bit forced.

jokes.text: Man, do you know what guys want.
jokes.setup: Rachel listing Erin's qualities: speaks four languages
jokes.punchline: Joey sarcastically suggesting that language skills are what men find attractive
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm from Joey that shows he understands male psychology better than Rachel thinks. Well-delivered putdown.

jokes.text: My beers?!
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning Erin had two beers at lunch
jokes.punchline: Joey's priorities - he's more concerned about his beer than her drinking habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - missing the point about compatibility to focus on his beer being consumed. Great character priority joke.

jokes.text: You're in love with your assistant. You're having an affair with the guy who keeps pigeons on the roof.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Monica judging Joey for not wanting to date Erin
jokes.punchline: Joey firing back with their own relationship secrets
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and counter-attack from Joey. The pigeon detail is absurd enough to be funny.

jokes.text: Phoebe! Secret affair.
jokes.setup: Joey revealing Phoebe's secret affair
jokes.punchline: Monica's shocked reaction and Phoebe's deadpan correction about it being secret
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's casual attitude about secrets. The timing of the correction works well.

jokes.text: What did I say?
jokes.setup: Phoebe just revealed her secret affair and everyone's staring
jokes.punchline: She genuinely doesn't understand what the problem is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe obliviousness. Her innocent confusion after dropping a bombshell is very in character.

jokes.text: She was shocked, but then again, so were most people.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if Janice freaked out about their engagement
jokes.punchline: Monica's self-aware admission that everyone was shocked by their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about Monica and Chandler's unexpected pairing. Shows character growth and awareness.

jokes.text: How hard is that? 'Your check's in the mail.' 'Oh, your baby's so cute.' 'I can't wait to read your book, Ross.'
jokes.setup: Monica asking how hard it is to lie
jokes.punchline: Chandler's examples escalate from generic lies to a specific dig at Ross's boring book
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with a perfect character-specific punchline. The Ross book reference is both funny and cruel, very Chandler.

jokes.text: You don't think she'll want to sing 'Part-Time Lover'?
jokes.setup: Discussing whether Janice will cause problems at their wedding
jokes.punchline: Reference to Janice's inappropriate song choice for a wedding
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Janice's tone-deaf social behavior. The song choice is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: Really? No, that was a lie. See how easy that was?
jokes.setup: Monica hopefully asking if Janice won't want to come to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Chandler demonstrates lying in real-time, proving his earlier point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that demonstrates the concept while delivering it. Perfect structure and economy. Classic Chandler wit.

jokes.text: People are doing it in front of my book.
jokes.setup: Monica asking what's wrong
jokes.punchline: Ross's academic outrage at people having sex near his dissertation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross academic narcissism - making other people's sex about his scholarly work. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Did you get to see anything good?
jokes.setup: Ross complaining about people having sex at the library
jokes.punchline: Monica's priorities shift from sympathy to voyeuristic curiosity
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Monica's unexpected reaction. The shift from concern to curiosity is funny.

jokes.text: That's not the point. I know that's not the point. I'm trying to make it the point.
jokes.setup: Ross pointing out Monica missed the point of his complaint
jokes.punchline: Monica's honest admission that she's trying to redirect to the salacious details
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay showing Monica's unashamed nosiness vs Ross's academic concerns.

jokes.text: In the corner of the library where all the books were that nobody ever read.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining where students went to fool around at her school
jokes.punchline: The perfect location was among books nobody cared about
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about academic hierarchy - unused books become make-out spots. Good real-world logic.

jokes.text: Oh, for crying out loud!
jokes.setup: Realizing his book is in the exact spot Monica described
jokes.punchline: Ross's dismay at discovering his work is in the 'nobody reads' section
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup payoff - Ross realizes his book is so unpopular it's in the hookup section. Good character deflation.

jokes.text: Oh, no. What party? A birthday party. A birthday party. Whose birthday party? Alison's birthday party. Alison's birthday party.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Phoebe trying to create fake excuse to avoid double date
jokes.punchline: Their completely uncoordinated, obvious lie-making in real time
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy with the painfully obvious coordination. Works through awkwardness rather than wit.

jokes.text: And how old is Alison? Thirty-two. Thirty-two.
jokes.setup: Joey testing their fake story
jokes.punchline: They continue to obviously coordinate their lie
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the bad lying sequence. Less impact than the initial setup but maintains the cringe.

jokes.text: Look how that worked out.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Joey and Erin do something together
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic reference to their failed matchmaking
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic callback to their earlier failure. Economy of language makes it effective.

jokes.text: That, actually, might go late. I'll wait up.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying their fake party might run late
jokes.punchline: Joey's eager response ruins their excuse
jokes.explanation: Joey's oblivious enthusiasm undermining their plan. Good character consistency with his romantic eagerness.

jokes.text: Well, yes. Just give me five minutes. I just have to find someone to cover my shift.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about the paleontology section
jokes.punchline: Librarian assumes he wants to hook up there too
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - the librarian's eagerness reveals how common this request is. Good cringe setup.

jokes.text: Don't you follow me.
jokes.setup: Ross declaring he'll defend his book himself
jokes.punchline: He warns the librarian not to follow him to the make-out spot
jokes.explanation: Good Ross moment showing his oblivious self-importance. The warning suggests he thinks he's irresistible.

jokes.text: Didn't you sleep together? Yeah, that calms me down.
jokes.setup: Joey saying he was nervous on the first date
jokes.punchline: His casual attitude about sex as a calming mechanism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey sexual philosophy - treating intimacy as casual stress relief. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Not an issue now, but if we had kids...
jokes.setup: Joey mentioning Erin is a Mets fan
jokes.punchline: He's already thinking about their hypothetical children's team loyalty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Joey jumping from dating to kids over sports teams. Realistic male sports obsession.

jokes.text: Oh, my Joey. Please don't get married before I do.
jokes.setup: Joey talking about having kids with Erin
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's maternal reaction and competitive marriage anxiety
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Phoebe's protective feelings about Joey and her own romantic insecurities.

jokes.text: What are you, stupid? It's been three hours. Is that all?
jokes.setup: Chandler suggesting Clark might still show up
jokes.punchline: Janice's harsh realism followed by Chandler's sarcastic time observation
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Janice's bluntness vs Chandler's sarcastic timing observation.

jokes.text: I thought this guy was my soul mate. I thought I was your soul mate.
jokes.setup: Janice lamenting about Clark being her soul mate
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward reminder of their past relationship
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment highlighting the weirdness of comforting an ex. Chandler's neediness shows through.

jokes.text: Do you want me to sing 'Careless Whisper' or 'Lady in Red'?
jokes.setup: Monica trying to uninvite Janice from the wedding
jokes.punchline: Janice completely missing the hint and offering inappropriate song choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice obliviousness and terrible song choices. Both songs are completely wrong for weddings, which is perfect for her character.

jokes.text: Second? Who's the first? Me and this muffin. My God, it's delicious.
jokes.setup: Phoebe calling Joey and Erin the second-greatest love affair
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's love affair is with her muffin
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and very Phoebe to prioritize food over romance. The genuine enthusiasm sells it.

jokes.text: Then change it back!
jokes.setup: Erin saying she changed her mind about liking Joey
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate demand that she reverse her feelings
jokes.explanation: Good desperate moment from Rachel showing her investment in the matchmaking. The command is absurd but in character.

jokes.text: Didn't you sleep together? Yeah. Tramp.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if Erin slept with Joey despite not liking him
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate judgmental reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's quick judgment shift. The blunt 'tramp' is funny in its directness.

jokes.text: I guess Italian isn't one of the four languages you speak.
jokes.setup: Erin asking what 'stronzo' means
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic dig at Erin's claimed language skills
jokes.explanation: Good callback that weaponizes earlier information. Shows Rachel turning mean when her plans fail.

jokes.text: No, just a regular old flying dwarf.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying it wasn't Cupid who brought Erin
jokes.punchline: Rachel's bitter description of Cupid as a 'regular old flying dwarf'
jokes.explanation: Creative deflation of Cupid mythology. The specific 'flying dwarf' description is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: Get out of here!
jokes.setup: Students pretending to be scholarly to access the make-out spot
jokes.punchline: Ross's immediate, aggressive ejection of them
jokes.explanation: Good Ross academic protectiveness. His immediate hostility to fake scholars is very in character.

jokes.text: I find Merriam's views far too progressionist.
jokes.setup: Ross testing someone he thinks is faking scholarship
jokes.punchline: She gives a sophisticated academic response
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Ross (and audience) expect fake scholarship but get the real thing. Sets up his romantic interest.

jokes.text: You're the person who checked out my book?
jokes.setup: Ross finding someone who actually read his dissertation
jokes.punchline: His amazed realization that someone actually checked out his book
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross academic insecurity and desperation for validation. The singular 'person' suggests only one checkout ever.

jokes.text: Well, I skipped fourth grade.
jokes.setup: Ross commenting that she looks young for someone so scholarly
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact explanation for her academic advancement
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that establishes her academic credentials while being modest about them.

jokes.text: Seventy-thirty, but still.
jokes.setup: Joey describing sharing a milkshake with Erin
jokes.punchline: He admits to drinking most of it but still counts it as romantic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey selfishness disguised as romance. The specific ratio and 'but still' shows his self-awareness and lack of shame.

jokes.text: Hey, Rach, it's cool, you know. I'm a loner too, right?
jokes.setup: Rachel telling Joey that Erin said she's a loner
jokes.punchline: Joey embracing the loner identity he wanted to project earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey trying to make the best of rejection by embracing his earlier persona.

jokes.text: Next time, make sure she likes me.
jokes.setup: Rachel promising to tell future dates he's not looking for relationships
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple, practical counter-request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - cutting through the complexity to the simple solution. Shows emotional growth through hurt.

jokes.text: Do you want some pancakes? Finally.
jokes.setup: Joey looking dejected after the Erin situation
jokes.punchline: Rachel finally offers the pancakes Joey wanted all episode
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback that provides comfort food resolution to Joey's rejection. His 'finally' shows he never forgot.

jokes.text: What are we gonna do? I say we go with 'Careless Whisper.'
jokes.setup: Monica asking what they'll do about Janice
jokes.punchline: Chandler accepting defeat and choosing her wedding song
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Chandler's resignation. The specific song choice makes it funnier than generic acceptance.

jokes.text: Our kids are gonna call her Aunt Janice, aren't they?
jokes.setup: Janice asking to stay and calling them family
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horrified realization of the long-term implications
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that jumps from one night to lifelong family implications. Very relatable relationship anxiety.

jokes.text: Kind of like your Barcalounger.
jokes.setup: Monica saying if they let Janice stay she'll stay forever
jokes.punchline: Reference to Chandler's apparently permanent chair
jokes.explanation: Good callback that equates Janice with unwanted furniture. Shows Monica's priorities and ongoing domestic issues.

jokes.text: I never stop thinking about it.
jokes.setup: Monica comparing Janice to his Barcalounger
jokes.punchline: Chandler's obsessive attachment to his chair
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's attachment to comfort items. The intensity is funny given it's just a chair.

jokes.text: Chandler, she has beaten me. I'm tired, Chandler. So very tired!
jokes.setup: The struggle to deal with Janice
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic surrender in war movie style
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic escalation turning domestic annoyance into epic defeat. Monica's exhaustion is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Our apartment is a hotbed for electromagnetic activity. Monica and I have been immunized, but sadly, you have not.
jokes.setup: Needing to get rid of Janice
jokes.punchline: Chandler's ridiculous scientific excuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist excuse with pseudo-scientific language. The 'immunized' detail makes it more elaborate and funny.

jokes.text: Because Chandler still has feelings for you.
jokes.setup: Janice not buying the electromagnetic excuse
jokes.punchline: Monica switching to an emotional lie that works
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from absurd to emotional manipulation. Works because it plays to Janice's ego and romantic delusions.

jokes.text: Feelings. Such strong feelings.
jokes.setup: Monica's lie about Chandler having feelings
jokes.punchline: Her over-selling the emotional intensity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the lie with repetitive emphasis. Monica's amateur acting skills make it more believable to Janice.

jokes.text: You call me when this goes in the pooper.
jokes.setup: Janice leaving after being told about Chandler's feelings
jokes.punchline: Her crude but somehow sweet parting words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice line - crude but caring, inappropriate but well-meaning. The specific phrase 'in the pooper' is very her.

jokes.text: I just wanted to show Monica your book.
jokes.setup: Kristin appearing at Monica's restaurant
jokes.punchline: Her innocent reason for being there with Ross's book
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending that validates Ross's academic work. Not particularly funny but provides nice character resolution.

tmdb_overview: Rachel and Phoebe befriend Joey's girlfriend. Ross finds his dissertation in the library. Monica runs into Janice who invites herself to the wedding.
